metadata.title: A2 Key Gold Mock 1
metadata.description: M3.1 academic reference mock for A2 Key for Schools — full reading & writing, listening, and speaking form.
metadata.authoringNotes: M4.1 Gold Mock — manually authored and academically reviewed.

parts.title: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct answer
parts.instructions: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct answer

audio.transcript: Woman: I'd like six of those red apples, please. Shop assistant: Here you are. That's three pounds.
audio.caption: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct answer

parts.title: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer
parts.instructions: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

audio.transcript: Boy: Sorry I'm late, Miss. My alarm didn't go off this morning. Teacher: Please try to be on time tomorrow.
audio.caption: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

parts.title: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap
parts.instructions: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Guide: Welcome to the city museum. We open at nine o'clock. Meet your group at the library entrance at half past eight.
audio.caption: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Speaker: My favourite animals are dolphins because they are intelligent and friendly. I first saw them on a family holiday.
audio.caption: Part 4 — Listen to the monologue

parts.title: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity
parts.instructions: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity

audio.transcript: Coordinator: On Monday we have drama club, on Tuesday basketball, Wednesday choir, Thursday coding, and Friday gardening.
audio.caption: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity

questions.questionText: NO ENTRY — Staff only beyond this point.

choices.text: Visitors may go through if they ask.
Only workers can go past this door.
The exit is closed for cleaning.

questions.questionText: Library closed for repairs until 15 March.

choices.text: The library will open again after 15 March.

questions.questionText: Café special: Buy one sandwich, get a free drink.

choices.text: You receive a drink at no extra cost with a sandwich.

questions.questionText: Parents' evening — Thursday 6 p.m. Please book online.

choices.text: Parents must make an appointment on the website.
The meeting is only for teachers.

questions.questionText: Sports hall in use — basketball training until 5 p.m.

choices.text: You can play football there now.
The hall is busy because of basketball practice.

questions.questionText: Flight delayed — new departure time 18:40, Gate 12.

choices.text: Passengers should go to Gate 12 later than planned.

questions.questionText: Who enjoys painting landscapes?

pairs.rightText: paints landscapes in her free time

questions.questionText: Who trains for swimming competitions?
Who is learning to build mobile apps?

pairs.rightText: is taking an online coding course

questions.questionText: Who played guitar in a school concert last term?
Who volunteers at an animal rescue centre?
Who is going to start a cooking blog?

pairs.rightText: plans to share family recipes online

questions.questionText: Who has organised a neighbourhood clean-up day?

pairs.rightText: has invited local families to collect litter

questions.questionText: What time did the class arrive at the museum?

content.passage: Last Saturday, our class visited the city science museum. We arrived at ten o'clock and met our guide, Mr Patel. First, we watched a short film about planets. Then we tried interactive experiments in the energy room. My favourite part was the robot workshop, where we programmed a small robot to move in a square. At lunchtime, we ate sandwiches in the café and talked about what we had learned. Before we left, the museum shop sold postcards and science kits. Everyone agreed it was an interesting and fun trip.

questions.questionText: What did the students do in the robot workshop?

content.passage: Last Saturday, our class visited the city science museum. We arrived at ten o'clock and met our guide, Mr Patel. First, we watched a short film about planets. At lunchtime, we ate sandwiches in the café and talked about what we had learned. Before we left, the museum shop sold postcards and science kits. Everyone agreed it was an interesting and fun trip.

choices.text: They programmed a robot to move in a square.
They watched a film about robots.

questions.questionText: Gap (1): natural ___

content.passage: Many teenagers enjoy learning English through films and music. When you watch a film in English, you can hear natural (1) ___ and learn new expressions. Some students write down useful phrases and (2) ___ them later. Reading short stories is also helpful because you see grammar in (3) ___. Online language apps can remind you to study every day and track your (4) ___. However, the best way to improve is to use English regularly with other (5) ___. Joining a conversation club at school gives you a chance to speak without (6) ___.

questions.questionText: We took ___ train to the coast.

content.template: We took [0] train to the coast.

questions.questionText: It ___ raining when we arrived.
She is interested ___ learning Spanish.
Walking is ___ than driving in the city centre.

content.template: Walking is [0] than driving in the city centre.

questions.questionText: The woman ___ works in the bookshop is my aunt.

content.template: The woman [0] works in the bookshop is my aunt.

questions.questionText: We ___ recycle plastic bottles at school.

content.template: We [0] recycle plastic bottles at school.

questions.questionText: Write an email to your English teacher.

content.prompt: Your English class is going on a trip to the theatre next Friday. Write an email to your teacher.
content.taskDescription: Write 25–35 words. Include all three points.
requiredPoints: Say whether you can come on the trip
Explain how you will travel to the theatre
Ask one question about the visit

questions.questionText: Write a story about a lost dog.

content.prompt: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story. Your story must begin with this sentence: 'The dog ran into the park.'

questions.questionText: What is the woman buying?
Where are the children waiting?
What sport are they playing?
What is the man doing in the kitchen?
What is the weather like?
Why was the boy late for school?
How often does Anna go to the gym?
Where are they going on holiday?
What has Tom already finished?
What does the doctor tell the patient to do?
The museum opens at ___ o'clock.
Meet me ___ the library entrance.
The film starts at quarter ___ seven.

content.template: The film starts at quarter [0] seven.

questions.questionText: Please bring a ___ of water.
She ___ as a nurse in the local hospital.

content.template: She [0] as a nurse in the local hospital.

choices.text: To meet people with similar interests

questions.questionText: Which activity takes place on Monday?
Which activity takes place on Tuesday?
Which activity takes place on Wednesday?
Which activity takes place on Thursday?
Which activity takes place on Friday?
Answer the examiner's questions about you and your daily life.

content.prompt: The examiner will ask you about yourself, your family, and your free time.
followUpQuestions: What do you enjoy doing at the weekend?
Tell me about your best friend.

questions.questionText: Read the scene description and describe what you see.

content.prompt: Read the scene description. Describe what you can see and say how the people might be feeling.
content.taskDescription: Scene: A group of teenagers are in a city park on a sunny afternoon. Some are sitting on benches talking, others are playing with a ball near a fountain.

coverageAchieved.notes: Authored for M3.1 Gold Mock specification.
